q: A silicon sample is doped with 10^16 donor atoms per cm^3. If the intrinsic carrier concentration ni is 1.5 x 10^10 per cm^3, calculate the concentration of holes in this extrinsic sample at room temperature.
An extrinsic germanium crystal at room temperature (ni = 2.5 x 10^13 per cm^3) has a hole concentration of 5 x 10^11 per cm^3. Find the electron concentration in this crystal.

Calculate the electrical conductivity of an N-type silicon sample with an electron concentration of 10^16 per cm^3. Assume electron mobility is 1350 cm^2/V-s and the electron charge is 1.6 x 10^-19 Coulombs. (Ignore holes)
Find the resistivity of a P-type silicon semiconductor doped with an acceptor concentration of 5 x 10^15 per cm^3. Assume hole mobility is 450 cm^2/V-s and hole charge is 1.6 x 10^-19 Coulombs.
Calculate the diffusion coefficient (Dn) of electrons in silicon at room temperature (T = 300 K, thermal voltage V_T = 25.9 mV) if the electron mobility is 1200 cm^2/V-s.
Calculate the diffusion coefficient (Dp) of holes in a semiconductor sample where the hole mobility is 400 cm^2/V-s at a room temperature thermal voltage of 25.9 mV.

Find the built-in potential V_bi of a silicon PN junction at room temperature (V_T = 25.9 mV) if the donor doping N_D is 10^15 per cm^3 and the acceptor doping N_A is 10^17 per cm^3. (Use ni = 1.5 x 10^10 per cm^3)
A germanium PN junction (ni = 2.5 x 10^13 per cm^3) has doping concentrations N_D = 10^16 per cm^3 and N_A = 10^16 per cm^3. Calculate the built-in potential at 300 K (V_T = 25.9 mV).
An ideal silicon diode at room temperature (V_T = 26 mV) has a reverse saturation current of 10 pA. Calculate the diode current when a forward bias voltage of 0.65 Volts is applied.
Calculate the forward bias voltage across an ideal silicon diode (reverse saturation current Is = 1 pA) at room temperature (V_T = 25.9 mV) when the forward current flowing through it is 2.5 mA.
If the forward current of a diode increases by a factor of 10, find the corresponding increase in its forward voltage drop at room temperature (V_T = 26 mV, ideality factor n = 1).

A Zener diode regulator circuit consists of a 12-Volt unregulated DC source, a 150 Ohm series current-limiting resistor, and a 5.1-Volt Zener diode. Calculate the current flowing through the Zener diode when there is no load connected.

For the same Zener regulator circuit (12 V supply, 150 Ohm series resistor, 5.1 V Zener), a load resistor of 250 Ohms is connected in parallel with the Zener diode. Find the new current through the Zener diode.
In the previous regulator circuit, what is the minimum load resistance (RL) required to keep the Zener diode in the breakdown state? (Assume the minimum required Zener current is 5 mA).
A common-emitter BJT has a base current of 50 uA and a collector current of 4.5 mA. Calculate the common-emitter current gain (beta) of this BJT.
For the same BJT with IB = 50 uA and IC = 4.5 mA, calculate the value of its common-base current gain (alpha).
If a transistor has an alpha of 0.995, calculate its common-emitter current gain (beta).
Calculate the emitter current (IE) of a transistor that has a beta of 120 and a base current of 25 uA.

A certain transistor has a collector current of 10 mA and a base current of 100 uA. Calculate the leakage current IC (approximate) if the collector-emitter leakage with base open ICEO is 2 uA. (Find base-to-collector current amplification beta).
Calculate the dynamic forward resistance of a PN junction diode carrying a forward DC current of 5 mA at room temperature (V_T = 26 mV, n = 1).
An N-channel JFET has IDSS = 12 mA and V_P = -4 Volts. Calculate the drain current (ID) when the gate-to-source voltage V_GS is -1 Volt.
For the JFET in the previous question (IDSS = 12 mA, V_P = -4 V), calculate the drain current when V_GS is -2 Volts.
Calculate the maximum transconductance (gm0) of an N-channel JFET at V_GS = 0 V if IDSS = 16 mA and V_P = -4 Volts.

Using the maximum transconductance gm0 = 8 mS and pinch-off voltage V_P = -4 V from the previous question, calculate the transconductance gm when V_GS is -1 Volt. (Context from previous question: For the JFET in the previous question (IDSS = 12 mA, V_P = -4 V), calculate the drain current when V_GS is -2 Volts. An N-channel JFET has IDSS = 12 mA and V_P = -4 Volts.)
An N-channel enhancement-mode MOSFET has a threshold voltage V_th = 2 Volts and conduction parameter K = 0.5 mA/V^2. Find the drain current in saturation when V_GS = 4 Volts.
For the MOSFET in the previous question (V_th = 2 V, K = 0.5 mA/V^2), what is the saturation drain current when V_GS is 6 Volts?

A certain silicon PN junction at zero bias has a depletion capacitance of 50 pF. If the built-in potential V_bi is 0.7 Volts, calculate the depletion capacitance when a reverse bias of 2.1 Volts is applied. (Assume junction exponent m = 0.5).
An SCR in a circuit is turned on. It has a holding current of 10 mA and a latching current of 25 mA. If the anode current falls to 8 mA, what happens to the SCR?
a: It stays in full conduction state
c: It enters a high-frequency oscillatory state
d: The gate current must be reversed

q: An SCR is triggered by a gate pulse. If the latching current of the SCR is 30 mA and the gate trigger pulse width is very narrow, what is the minimum load current required to keep the SCR on after the gate pulse ends?

A UJT circuit has an intrinsic stand-off ratio (eta) of 0.6. If the interbase voltage V_BB is 15 Volts and the forward diode drop is 0.7 Volts, calculate the peak-point voltage (V_P) required to trigger the UJT.
For the UJT in the previous question, if the interbase resistance R_BB is 10k Ohms and eta is 0.6, calculate the base-1 resistance (R_B1) under non-conducting conditions.

Calculate the reverse recovery charge (Q_rr) stored in a fast recovery diode if the reverse recovery time (trr) is 50 nano-seconds and the peak reverse current (Irr) is 4 Amperes. (Assume a triangular recovery waveform shape).
A certain silicon wafer is doped with 10^16 boron atoms per cm^3. Find the majority carrier concentration in this sample at 300 K.
Find the resistivity of intrinsic silicon at room temperature (300 K) if ni = 1.5 x 10^10 per cm^3, electron mobility = 1350 cm^2/V-s, and hole mobility = 450 cm^2/V-s.
In a semiconductor sample, the electron concentration profile decreases linearly from 10^18 per cm^3 to 10^15 per cm^3 over a distance of 2 micro-meters. Find the electron concentration gradient.
Using the concentration gradient from the previous question (-4.995 x 10^21 per cm^4), calculate the diffusion current density (Jn) of electrons if the diffusion constant Dn is 25 cm^2/s.

A silicon PN junction has doping concentrations N_A = 10^18 per cm^3 and N_D = 10^15 per cm^3. If the built-in potential is 0.75 Volts, calculate the depletion region width (W) at zero bias. (Use permittivity of silicon epsilon_s = 1.04 x 10^-12 F/cm).
For the same silicon PN junction, calculate the depletion width under a reverse bias voltage of 5.25 Volts. (Total potential = V_bi + V_R = 6.0 Volts).
A certain diode has a reverse saturation current of 5 pA. Find the ratio of forward current at 0.7 Volts forward bias to the current at 0.6 Volts forward bias at room temperature (V_T = 26 mV).
An ideal silicon diode operates at 300 K. If the forward voltage is 0.6 Volts when the current is 1 mA, calculate the forward voltage when the current increases to 10 mA.

Calculate the reverse breakdown voltage of a diode if the maximum electric field is 3 x 10^5 V/cm, the donor concentration is 10^15 per cm^3, and the acceptor concentration is 10^18 per cm^3. (Use breakdown formula V_B = epsilon_s * E_max^2 / (2 * q * N_D)).

A common-emitter amplifier has a collector resistor RC = 2k Ohms and operates from a 15-Volt DC source. If the transistor is in saturation and V_CE(sat) is 0.2 Volts, calculate the saturation collector current.

If the transistor in the previous question has a minimum beta of 50, calculate the minimum base current (IB) required to guarantee saturation. If the transistor is in saturation and V_CE(sat) is 0.2 Volts, calculate the saturation collector current.)
A BJT is biased in the active region with IC = 2 mA and IB = 15 uA. Calculate the collector-to-emitter current gain beta.
For the same BJT (IC = 2 mA, IB = 15 uA), calculate the total emitter current.
An N-channel JFET is biased at V_GS = -1.5 Volts. If IDSS = 10 mA and V_P = -3.0 Volts, calculate the drain current ID.

What is the transconductance gm of the JFET in the previous question if gm0 is 6.67 mS? (Context from previous question: An N-channel JFET is biased at V_GS = -1.5 Volts. If IDSS = 10 mA and V_P = -3.0 Volts, calculate the drain current ID.)
An N-channel JFET has IDSS = 8 mA and V_P = -4 Volts. Find the drain-to-source voltage (V_DS) required to pinch off the channel when V_GS = -1 Volt.
For the JFET operating in the saturation region with V_GS = -1 Volt (IDSS = 8 mA, V_P = -4 V), calculate the drain current ID.
An enhancement-mode NMOS has V_th = 1.5 Volts and K = 0.25 mA/V^2. Find the drain current ID in saturation when the applied gate-to-source voltage V_GS is 3.5 Volts.

If the drain-to-source voltage V_DS of the NMOS in the previous question is 1.0 Volt, verify if it operates in saturation or linear region. (V_GS = 3.5 V, V_th = 1.5 V). Find the drain current ID using the linear equation.
A PIN photodiode has a quantum efficiency of 80% at an illumination wavelength of 0.8 micro-meters. Calculate the responsivity of the photodiode.

If the incident optical power on the photodiode described in the previous question is 5 micro-Watts, calculate the generated photocurrent. (Context from previous question: A PIN photodiode has a quantum efficiency of 80% at an illumination wavelength of 0.8 micro-meters.)

An SCR has a gate trigger voltage of 0.8 Volts and a gate trigger current of 15 mA. If it is triggered using a 10-Volt DC trigger supply, calculate the series resistor value required in the gate circuit.

A UJT relaxation oscillator has R = 10k Ohms and C = 0.1 micro-Farads. If the intrinsic stand-off ratio is 0.63, calculate the approximate period of oscillation. (Use formula T = R * C * ln(1 / (1 - eta))).

Calculate the frequency of oscillation for the UJT relaxation oscillator described in the previous question. (Context from previous question: A UJT relaxation oscillator has R = 10k Ohms and C = 0.1 micro-Farads. If the intrinsic stand-off ratio is 0.63, calculate the approximate period of oscillation. In a semiconductor sample, the electron concentration profile decreases linearly from 10^17 per cm^3 to 10^14 per cm^3 over a distance of 1 micro-meter. Find the electron concentration gradient.
Using the concentration gradient from the previous question (-9.9 x 10^20 per cm^4), calculate the diffusion current density (Jn) of electrons if the diffusion constant Dn is 20 cm^2/s.

A silicon PN junction has doping concentrations N_A = 10^17 per cm^3 and N_D = 10^16 per cm^3. If the built-in potential is 0.72 Volts, calculate the depletion region width (W) at zero bias. (Use permittivity = 1.04 x 10^-12 F/cm).
For the same silicon PN junction, calculate the depletion width under a reverse bias voltage of 5.28 Volts. (Total potential = V_bi + V_R = 6.0 Volts).
A certain diode has a reverse saturation current of 10 pA. Find the ratio of forward current at 0.65 Volts forward bias to the current at 0.55 Volts forward bias at room temperature (V_T = 26 mV).
An ideal silicon diode operates at 300 K. If the forward voltage is 0.55 Volts when the current is 1 mA, calculate the forward voltage when the current increases to 10 mA.

Calculate the reverse breakdown voltage of a diode if the maximum electric field is 4 x 10^5 V/cm, the donor concentration is 10^16 per cm^3, and the acceptor concentration is 10^19 per cm^3. (Use breakdown formula V_B = epsilon_s * E_max^2 / (2 * q * N_D)).

A common-emitter amplifier has a collector resistor RC = 1k Ohms and operates from a 12-Volt DC source. If the transistor is in saturation and V_CE(sat) is 0.2 Volts, calculate the saturation collector current.

If the transistor in the previous question has a minimum beta of 100, calculate the minimum base current (IB) required to guarantee saturation. (Context from previous question: A common-emitter amplifier has a collector resistor RC = 1k Ohms and operates from a 12-Volt DC source. If the transistor is in saturation and V_CE(sat) is 0.2 Volts, calculate the saturation collector current.)
A BJT is biased in the active region with IC = 5 mA and IB = 40 uA. Calculate the collector-to-emitter current gain beta.
For the same BJT (IC = 5 mA, IB = 40 uA), calculate the total emitter current.
An N-channel JFET is biased at V_GS = -2.0 Volts. If IDSS = 18 mA and V_P = -6.0 Volts, calculate the drain current ID.

What is the transconductance gm of the JFET in the previous question if gm0 is 6.0 mS? (Context from previous question: An N-channel JFET is biased at V_GS = -2.0 Volts. If IDSS = 18 mA and V_P = -6.0 Volts, calculate the drain current ID.)
An N-channel JFET has IDSS = 10 mA and V_P = -5 Volts. Find the drain-to-source voltage (V_DS) required to pinch off the channel when V_GS = -2 Volts.
For the JFET operating in the saturation region with V_GS = -2 Volts (IDSS = 10 mA, V_P = -5 V), calculate the drain current ID.
An enhancement-mode NMOS has V_th = 2.0 Volts and K = 0.5 mA/V^2. Find the drain current ID in saturation when the applied gate-to-source voltage V_GS is 5.0 Volts.

If the drain-to-source voltage V_DS of the NMOS in the previous question is 1.5 Volts, find the drain current ID using the linear equation. (Context from previous question: An enhancement-mode NMOS has V_th = 2.0 Volts and K = 0.5 mA/V^2.)
A PIN photodiode has a quantum efficiency of 75% at an illumination wavelength of 0.9 micro-meters. Calculate the responsivity of the photodiode.

If the incident optical power on the photodiode described in the previous question is 10 micro-Watts, calculate the generated photocurrent. (Context from previous question: A PIN photodiode has a quantum efficiency of 75% at an illumination wavelength of 0.9 micro-meters.)

An SCR has a gate trigger voltage of 0.7 Volts and a gate trigger current of 10 mA. If it is triggered using a 5-Volt DC trigger supply, calculate the series resistor value required in the gate circuit.

A UJT relaxation oscillator has R = 22k Ohms and C = 0.01 micro-Farads. If the intrinsic stand-off ratio is 0.63, calculate the approximate period of oscillation. (Use formula T = R * C * ln(1 / (1 - eta))).

Calculate the frequency of oscillation for the UJT relaxation oscillator described in the previous question. (Context from previous question: A UJT relaxation oscillator has R = 22k Ohms and C = 0.01 micro-Farads. If the intrinsic stand-off ratio is 0.63, calculate the approximate period of oscillation. (Use formula T = R * C * ln(1 / (1 - eta))).)
A solar cell has an open-circuit voltage of 0.55 Volts and a short-circuit current of 200 mA. If the fill factor of the solar cell is 0.70, calculate the maximum power output.

Calculate the efficiency of the solar cell described in the previous question if the total incident light power on the cell is 800 mW. (Context from previous question: A solar cell has an open-circuit voltage of 0.55 Volts and a short-circuit current of 200 mA. If the fill factor of the solar cell is 0.70, calculate the maximum power output.)
A silicon sample contains 10^16 donor atoms/cm^3 and 2 x 10^15 acceptor atoms/cm^3. What is the net carrier type and concentration?
Calculate the resistivity of P-type silicon doped with 2 x 10^16 acceptor atoms per cm^3. Assume hole mobility is 450 cm^2/V-s. (Use q = 1.6 x 10^-19 C).
